ollama.name: ollama
ollama.purpose: Baseline local model server and current default runtime.

mlx.name: mlx
mlx.purpose: Apple Silicon optimized MLX/MLX-LM server lane for benchmarking before promotion.

planner.purpose: Architecture, step-by-step reasoning, tradeoff analysis, and project planning

coder.purpose: Writing, editing, refactoring, and repository-scale coding with Qwen3.6 35B-A3B in content-generation mode

writer.purpose: Drafting social posts, community updates, content calendars, and public-facing copy

autocomplete.purpose: Short local code completion and small implementation prompts without retaining Qwen 2.5 models

notes: Keep private trading logic, exchange keys, wallet keys, and deployment keys out of prompts unless explicitly approved.
Use the planner for high-level reasoning, the coder for concrete file edits, and the writer for social/content drafts.
Autocomplete now uses Qwen3.6 with think:false so Qwen 2.5 models can be removed from the local machine.
Qwen3.6 35B-A3B is configured as the coder target with Ollama think:false so benchmark/test requests return content instead of spending the whole output cap on thinking text.
Qwen3-Coder-Next remains installed as the coder fallback/heavy comparison model.

MLX benchmark result: mlx-community/Qwen3-Coder-Next-4bit was faster than Ollama qwen3-coder-next:latest in local tests, but the MLX server must be running on port 8080 and crashed under concurrent large-model memory pressure.
